hypotheses_results.hypothesis: H1: The treatment reduces symptom severity scores significantly compared to placebo
hypotheses_results.explanation: Treatment group showed a mean change of -24.6 vs placebo -7.3, a difference of 17.3 points. Two-sample t-test on change scores is highly significant with a large effect size.

hypotheses_results.hypothesis: H2: The treatment achieves remission in more than 30% of patients
hypotheses_results.explanation: Remission rate in treatment group is 39% (78/200), exceeding the 30% threshold. One-sample proportion test against 0.30 is significant.

hypotheses_results.hypothesis: H3: The side effect rate of the treatment is not significantly higher than placebo
hypotheses_results.explanation: Side effect rate is 26% in treatment vs 17.5% in placebo. Chi-square test of proportions shows a statistically significant difference, meaning the treatment does have a higher side effect rate.
